item.title: S&P 500 down 1.16%
item.description: The US stock market shows signs of weakness with S&P 500 down 1.16% to 6696.94.

item.title: Brent crude at $95.85
item.description: Brent crude oil price rises 4.21% to $95.85 due to the escalating Middle East conflict and concerns over oil production.

item.title: VIX at 26.19
item.description: The VIX index rises to 26.19, indicating increased risk aversion among investors due to uncertainty over the Middle East conflict.

item.title: TLT down 0.21%
item.description: Government bonds are in compression, with TLT down 0.21%.

item.title: Middle East conflict escalates
item.description: The joint U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran has led to Iranian retaliation, engulfing much of the Middle East in conflict.

item.title: Global inflation could rise
item.description: The rise in oil prices could lead to an increase in global inflation.

item.title: Oil prices rising
item.description: Oil prices are rising due to the escalating Middle East conflict and concerns over oil production.
